exercises.title: Inspect Broker Lag
exercises.description: Check the lag or depth of a queue and compare it with the active consumer count.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can see whether consumers are keeping up with producers.

exercises.title: Trace an Ack Path
exercises.description: Send one message and confirm the broker marks it acknowledged after processing.
exercises.expected_outcome: A processed message disappears from the in-flight set.

exercises.title: Build an Idempotent Consumer
exercises.description: Write a consumer that ignores duplicate message IDs and only applies side effects once.
exercises.expected_outcome: Retries do not duplicate the resulting state.

exercises.title: Implement a DLQ Path
exercises.description: Route messages that fail too many times into a dead-letter queue with their failure reason.
exercises.expected_outcome: Bad messages stop blocking healthy ones and land in a DLQ.

exercises.title: Build a Retryable Job Runner
exercises.description: Create a small async worker that pulls jobs, retries transient failures, and persists status.
exercises.expected_outcome: Jobs survive worker restarts and transient failures without losing progress.

exercises.title: Design an Event Pipeline
exercises.description: Model a producer, broker, consumer group, and DLQ for an end-to-end event flow.
exercises.expected_outcome: You end with a concrete design for message routing, retries, and failure handling.
